channel.title: GitHub Next
channel.description: A team of researchers and engineers at GitHub prototyping the tools, workflows, and ideas that will shape the future of software development.

item.title: Can agents be proud of their work?
item.description: Can agents be proud of their work?

item.title: Agentic Workflows Can Use Local Inference
item.description: Agentic Workflows gives you complete control to customize your Actions runtime and choose your AI model. You can deploy Agentic Workflows to a custom runner of your choosing and use any compatible AI host or model-routing platform. You can even use local inference running on your Mac!

item.title: /goooooooal!
item.description: Turn an issue into an agentic mission

item.title: Crane: verified code migration
item.description: Crane is a migration assistant for GitHub repositories that plans, executes, and verifies code migrations in small agentic steps while keeping humans in control.

item.title: Agentics Beyond Code
item.description: What happens when you give PMs, compliance teams, and leaders their own agents? A tour of Agentics Beyond Code — an open-source set of GitHub Agentic Workflows for the non-engineering roles that ship, govern, and operate products.

item.title: Control what your agentic workflows see with integrity filtering
item.description: GitHub Agentic Workflows filter untrusted GitHub content before it reaches the agent. Here’s why integrity filtering matters for repository maintainers, and how we built it.

item.title: Animating the native traffic lights in Ace
item.description: Animating the native traffic lights in Ace

item.title: Agent Functions
item.description: Prompts are programs. You wouldn’t write a complex program completely from scratch, in a big, soupy loop without subroutines, and then write it again the next time you wanted to run it. Why let your agent work that way?

item.title: A loader with level of detail
item.description: A loader with level of detail

item.title: Loading states that bleed outside the window
item.description: Loading states that bleed outside the window

item.title: Canary: a harm gate for agentic systems
item.description: Canary puts a small, auditable gate in front of agentic workflows so untrusted artifacts are classified before powerful agents act on them.

item.title: The Impact of Automated Repository Maintenance Assistance
item.description: The Impact of Automated Repository Maintenance Assistance

item.title: Agents are power tools
item.description: A practical mental model for agents, workflows, and human-machine systems in agentic engineering.

item.title: Agency is the New Resilience
item.description: Agents can power robust workflows by intelligently reacting to unexpected conditions, creating a new form of flexible resilience.

item.title: Understanding Repositories as Human/Agent Knowledge Factories
item.description: Understanding Repositories as Human/Agent Knowledge Factories

item.title: Autoloop is porting pandas to TypeScript
item.description: tsb is a from-scratch TypeScript port of pandas, being built almost entirely by Autoloop — one iterative improvement at a time.

item.title: Repo Mind Light
item.description: Holistic repository understanding for humans and agents.

item.title: New site, who dis?
item.description: New site, who dis?

item.title: Autoloop
item.description: Automate research, development and anything else with a simple, goal-driven loop

item.title: Lean Squad: Exploring Automated Software Verification with Near-Zero Human Labour
item.description: Lean Squad: Exploring Automated Software Verification with Near-Zero Human Labour

item.title: One Developer, Two Dozen Agents, Zero Alignment
item.description: Why we need collaborative AI engineering tools, and a tour of Ace — a research prototype from GitHub Next that brings teammates and coding agents into one shared, multiplayer workspace.

item.title: Repo Mind
item.description: Global codebase understanding for humans and AI agents.

item.title: Start Your Day With Code That’s Better
item.description: Start Your Day With Code That’s Better

item.title: Adding Weighted Task Selection to a GitHub Agentic Workflow
item.description: Adding Weighted Task Selection to a GitHub Agentic Workflow

item.title: Repo Assist: Crunching the Technical Debt with GitHub Agentic Workflows
item.description: Repo Assist: Crunching the Technical Debt with GitHub Agentic Workflows

item.title: Automate repository tasks with GitHub Agentic Workflows
item.description: Automate repository tasks with GitHub Agentic Workflows

item.title: Generative AI and Changing Inputs
item.description: Generative AI and Changing Inputs

item.title: Towards Semi-automatic Agentic Performance Engineering
item.description: Towards Semi-automatic Agentic Performance Engineering

item.title: Intent, meet Toolchain
item.description: Intent, meet Toolchain

item.title: What Kind of Programming is Natural Language Programming?
item.description: What Kind of Programming is Natural Language Programming?

item.title: On Continuous AI for Test Improvement
item.description: On Continuous AI for Test Improvement

item.title: On Natural Language Programming
item.description: On Natural Language Programming

item.title: Agentic Workflows
item.description: Towards Natural‑Language Programming for GitHub Actions

item.title: Project Copernicus
item.description: Exploring LLM-powered navigation for your codebase

item.title: Continuous AI
item.description: Exploring LLM-powered automation in platform-based software collaboration

item.title: Introducing “Continuous AI”
item.description: Introducing “Continuous AI”

item.title: Discovery Agent
item.description: Agentic Setup, Build, and Testing of Repositories.

item.title: Extract, Edit, Apply
item.description: An exploration of a new category of assists for using natural language in software development.

item.title: Mosaic
item.description: Can we derive personalized design systems from sources of inspiration?

item.title: Learning Sandbox
item.description: Can we make it easy and fun to learn as we build? We’re exploring ways to create personalized, interactive learning environments that integrate into your daily workflow.

item.title: GitHub Spark
item.description: Can we enable anyone to create or adapt software for themselves, using AI and a fully-managed runtime?

item.title: Vitale
item.description: Live notebooks in VS Code for JavaScript/TypeScript, web development, and AI experimentation

item.title: Bringing the power of AI into your application
item.description: A practical introduction to integrating large language models into applications, covering prompt crafting, chaining AI queries, and UX considerations for building AI-powered tools. Also addresses how to measure the impact of AI changes and evaluate whether AI integration is right for a given application.

item.title: Copilot Workspace
item.description: An agentic dev environment, designed for everyday tasks.

item.title: Monaspace
item.description: An innovative superfamily of fonts for code. How can we make code more expressive in any editor?

item.title: Copilot Next Edit Suggestions
item.description: Can we improve Copilot code completion by suggesting the next logical change, wherever it is in your project?

item.title: Realtime GitHub
item.description: Multiplayer collaboration for your whole repo.

item.title: SpecLang
item.description: Can we develop software entirely in natural language, and let an AI-powered toolchain manage the implementation?

item.title: Code Atlas
item.description: How can we make LLM responses more robust and easier to understand by combining their fluid reasoning with rigid structure?

item.title: Designing for Intelligence
item.description: A look at the design and research principles behind building AI-powered products at GitHub Next, covering how to elicit context from users, structure prompts, measure result quality, and weave intelligence into product experiences. Shares practical lessons learned from prototyping and shipping tools like GitHub Copilot across the software development lifecycle.

item.title: GPT-4 with Calc
item.description: An exploration of using calculation generation to improve GPT-4’s capabilities for numeric reasoning.

item.title: Copilot for Docs
item.description: How would it feel to have an expert on hand at all times? We built a tool that uses relevant information from a project’s documentation to answer questions or explain concepts.

item.title: Copilot for Pull Requests
item.description: Pull requests are a central part of the GitHub user experience. Copilot for PRs brings the power of Copilot to the PR experience, to help you write better PR descriptions, and to help your team review and merge PRs faster.

item.title: Copilot Completions in the CLI
item.description: Ever having trouble remembering shell commands and flags for this or that? Ever wish you could just *say* what you want the shell to do? Don’t worry: we’re building GitHub Copilot assistance right into your terminal.

item.title: Copilot Voice
item.description: Write code without the keyboard. Difficulty typing? Use your voice to code without spelling things out by talking with GitHub Copilot.

item.title: Crafting Code Suggestions using Large Language Models
item.description: An exploration of why large language models for code completion present unique engineering challenges — developers don’t write code linearly, so models must bridge the gap between predictive capability and real-world usefulness. Covers prompt engineering strategies including content reordering and codebase linearization, using GitHub Copilot as a case study.

item.title: GitHub Copilot and techniques for improving code suggestions from large language models
item.description: An examination of how to improve the performance of a given LLM on a specific task without modifying the underlying model — a more accessible area of research than training from scratch. Focuses on techniques used to enhance GitHub Copilot’s suggestions and introduces an offline evaluation framework for early-stage experimentation.

item.title: Code Brushes
item.description: Can editing code feel more tactile, like painting with Photoshop brushes? We added a toolbox of brushes to our Visual Studio Code extension that can modify your code.

item.title: A Backstage Pass with GitHub Copilot (Universe 2022)
item.description: An inside look at the research and data behind GitHub Copilot, combining live demos with survey results from GitHub engineers and academic perspectives on AI pair programming. Presents findings from a controlled study showing Copilot users completed a coding task significantly faster than those without it.

item.title: GitHub Blocks
item.description: Reimagine repositories with custom, interactive blocks. Build rich documentation, enhance your workflows, and bring your codebase to life.

item.title: AI-assisted pair programming with GitHub Copilot
item.description: An introduction to GitHub Copilot as an AI pair programming tool and how it integrates into developer workflows to accelerate coding. Covers how the system suggests code in context, adapts to individual projects, and what this means for the future of software development.

item.title: Collaborative Workspaces
item.description: As we increasingly work together remotely, how might we unify our workflows to enable remote collaboration for developers? GitHub Next explores what “working together” means, beyond multiple cursors and a shared code editor.

item.title: GitHub Copilot Radar
item.description: GitHub Copilot Radar supports code navigation with a constantly updated view of _other_ code locations most relevant to the one you’re currently viewing or editing. As a jump-to-definition on steroids, it leverages both static analysis and GitHub Copilot’s neural reasoning to make sure developers get the background they need about the code they touch.

item.title: GitHub Copilot for *Your* Codebase
item.description: When editing, GitHub Copilot only knows about the contents of your current file and possibly a few other open tabs, rendering it blind to important type definitions, patterns and greater connections in your codebase. We want to let GitHub Copilot see your entire repo when it comes up with its suggestions.

item.title: Incremental CodeQL
item.description: The goal of the Incremental CodeQL project is to investigate the potential for incrementalization to improve the performance of CodeQL query evaluation. Instead of having to re-analyze the whole code base from scratch, incremental evaluation reuses the previously computed analysis results and only re-analyzes the parts of the code base that are affected by a program change. This approach can lead to significant performance speed-ups without the need to make any changes to the analysis implementation.

item.title: TestPilot
item.description: TestPilot takes the pain out of writing unit tests. It uses GitHub Copilot’s AI technology to suggest tests based on your existing code and documentation. Unlike many other tools, TestPilot generates readable tests with meaningful assertions, and it can iteratively improve its suggestions based on your feedback.

item.title: Bringing the Power of AI into your Editor with GitHub Copilot
item.description: A technical deep dive into how GitHub Copilot works as an advanced AI autocomplete system embedded directly in the IDE, drawing on the collective knowledge of millions of developers. Covers the design principles behind Copilot, how it communicates with its AI model, and how it integrates with various code editors.

item.title: How We Evaluate the Quality of Copilot
item.description: An overview of the methods and metrics used to assess the quality and effectiveness of GitHub Copilot’s code suggestions at scale. Covers how evaluation frameworks are designed to measure both objective output quality and developer productivity impact.

item.title: From AI for Code to AI for Coding
item.description: Large language models like OpenAI Codex have reached the capability to generate large chunks of coherent code, enabling a new class of developer tools including GitHub Copilot. Examines what work remains to transform a code-prediction model into a genuinely transformative programming assistant, and the obstacles along the way.

item.title: Productivity Assessment of Neural Code Completion
item.description: A study examining how neural code completion tools affect developer productivity by comparing user perceptions with objective usage metrics across a real-world user base. Finds that suggestion acceptance rate — rather than whether completions persist in the codebase over time — is the strongest driver of developers’ perceived productivity gains.

item.title: Bringing data into your repos using Flat Data
item.description: Flat Data simplifies working with datasets in git by making it easy to fetch data from anywhere on the web directly into your repositories for processing, analyzing, and versioning. Builds on “git scraping” techniques to enable new developer workflows around local datasets.

item.title: GitHub Copilot Labs
item.description: A VS Code extension for experimental applications of GitHub Copilot.

item.title: React Webview UI Toolkit for VS Code
item.description: An ongoing collaboration with Microsoft to bring React to their library, [Webview UI Toolkit for Visual Studio Code](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-webview-ui-toolkit)

item.title: Visualizing a Codebase
item.description: How can we “fingerprint” a codebase to see its structure at a glance? Let’s explore ways to automatically visualize a GitHub repo, and how that could be useful.

item.title: A Tour of F# Editor Tooling
item.description: A tour of the editor tooling ecosystem for F#, covering language server support, IDE integrations, and the developer experience across editors.

item.title: GitHub Copilot
item.description: A new way to build software

item.title: Flat Data
item.description: Flat explores how to make it easy to work with data in git and GitHub. It builds on the [“git scraping” approach pioneered by Simon Willison](https://simonwillison.net/2020/Oct/9/git-scraping/) to offer a simple pattern for bringing working datasets into your repositories and versioning them, because developing against local datasets is faster and easier than working with data over the wire.
